title: Coryphantha daimonoceras
taxonomy: Coryphantha daimonoceras is classified within the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae. More specifically, it is placed in the tribe Cacteae. Within the tribe, it is part of the genus Coryphantha. Further classification places it as Coryphantha daimonoceras specifically within the Cactaceae family.
uses: Coryphantha daimonoceras, a species of cactus, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant's tubercles and flowers are rich in alkaloids and other bioactive compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, Coryphantha daimonoceras is used to treat various ailments, including wounds, infections, and digestive issues. However, there is limited scientific evidence to support its effectiveness for these uses, and more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal applications. Further studies are required to isolate and characterize the plant's bioactive compounds and to assess their safety and efficacy.

preparations: Coryphantha daimonoceras is a species of cactus, and like other cacti, it has been used in herbal preparations due to its potential medicinal properties. intro: Coryphantha daimonoceras, also known as the "devil's head pincushion cactus," is a species of cactus that belongs to the Echinocereus and Coryphantha genera. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and other conditions. Bioactive constituents of Coryphantha daimonoceras include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Coryphantha daimonoceras include teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions and side effects.
